mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal: each 1% decline in PAYEMS from its 12-month peak predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
regime_hint: any regime with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with PAYEMS declines (Spearman rho > -0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by > 3% month-over-month, this interaction indicates demand destruction that cascades into negative equity returns within 5 trading days as forward guidance revisions propagate through earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 696,982 million, HOUST at 1,382 thousand as of 2023-01-01
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or demand shock regime
failure_condition: Joint decline events produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against direction_5d.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as margin expansion and earnings growth expectations rise in response to demonstrated production capacity utilization.
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth (Spearman rho < 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, the Federal Reserve faces a dual mandate conflict. Market participants anticipate policy reversal, triggering a sharp positive repricing of equity valuations over the next 60 trading days as rate-cut expectations crystallize.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.4% and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2023-01-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with emerging labor market softness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient between UNRATE change and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows positive acceleration when compared to the prior quarter. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% annualized rate, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions follow confirmed demand expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,198.091 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2022-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) serves as an always-on inverse predictor of equity returns. When UNRATE is below 3.7%, tight labor markets signal wage pressure and inflation persistence, constraining Fed policy flexibility and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as terminal rate expectations rise.
regime_hint: tight labor market regime with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with low UNRATE levels (Spearman rho > -0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 3.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, real rates become restrictively tight. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market participants anticipate imminent policy pivot toward accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 300.536 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2023-01-01
regime_hint: disinflation phase with restrictive policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term between CPIAUCSL deceleration and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
